description: Fix non-deterministic navigation in the side panel ConfirmationDialog when a confirmation item is removed (auto-timeout or responded to). Currently the dialog jumps to index 0 instead of advancing to the next item at the same position.

userStories.title: Fix ConfirmationDialog navigation on item removal
userStories.description: As a user reviewing pending confirmations in the side panel, I want the dialog to advance to the next confirmation at the same position when the current one is removed, so that I am not disoriented by unexpected jumps back to the first item.
acceptanceCriteria: When the currently-displayed confirmation is removed from the confirmations prop (e.g., auto-timeout), the dialog shows the item at the same index position (or clamps to the last item if the removed item was at the end)
When the user manually responds to a confirmation (removing it), the dialog advances correctly
Navigation via prev/next buttons still works correctly after item removal
A unit test verifies: given confirmations [A, B, C] with B displayed, removing B causes C to be displayed (not A)
A unit test verifies: given confirmations [A, B] with B displayed, removing B causes A to be displayed (clamp to last)
userStories.notes:
File: platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/ConfirmationDialog.tsx, lines 73-78.

The bug: The component tracks which confirmation to display via `currentId` state (line 73), initialized to null. When the tracked item is removed from confirmations prop (e.g., by useServerNotifications auto-timeout), findIndex returns -1, and the fallback `currentIndex === -1 ? 0 : currentIndex` snaps to index 0. This means if you're looking at item 3 of 5 and item 3 auto-times out, the dialog jumps to item 1.

Fix approach: Replace the simple fallback with position-aware advancement. Two options:

Option A (recommended — derive from numeric index): Track a `currentIndex` number in state instead of `currentId`. Clamp it on render: `const safeIndex = Math.min(currentIndex, confirmations.length - 1)`. The prev/next buttons adjust the numeric index. When an item is removed, the index stays the same (pointing to the next item that slid into its position), or clamps to the end if it was the last item.

Option B (keep currentId, add useEffect): Keep currentId but add a useEffect that detects when currentId is no longer in confirmations, and updates it to the item at the same index position.

Option A is simpler and avoids the extra effect. The prev/next onClick handlers (lines 158, 164) would change to `setCurrentIndex(i => i - 1)` and `setCurrentIndex(i => i + 1)`. Disable conditions: `disabled={safeIndex === 0}` and `disabled={safeIndex >= count - 1}` (same as current).

The onRespond callback is called with the confirmation's id (not index), so that does not need to change.
